memoryArtifacts.headline: George Lanphere c.1638 - 1731
memoryArtifacts.description:
About George Lanphere (Lanphear)
Sharon Fehr:
GOOGLE "Eve of St. Bartholomew Massacre."
Page 6
PETER BUTTON[7] married MARY LANPHEAR [7]

Her father was GEORGE LANPHEAR [6], many spellings, probably f …

memoryArtifacts.headline: The Settlers of the Beekman Patent VII - The Lanphere Family, George Lanphere
memoryArtifacts.description: Provides details on George his wife Jane Hulet and their children. It also provides details on Seth Lanphere their son and his wife Sarah Pendleton and their children.

nameMeanings.name: Lamphere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of   Lanfear  .

variants.name: Lanfear
meanings: Welsh and English: habitational name from any of the many places in Wales called Llanfair ‘church of Saint Mary’, from Welsh llan ‘church’ + Mair ‘Mary’, for example Llanfair Caereinion (Montgomeryshire). Compare Lamphear , Lamphere , Lamphier , Lanfair , Lanphear , Lanpher , Lanphere , and Lanphier .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Pirates Hung in Newport
historicEvents.description: On July 17, 1723, twenty-eight pirates are hung in Newport, Rhode Island.

historicEvents.title: First Synagogue in America
historicEvents.description: The first Jewish Synagogue in America was built in Newport, Rhode Island in 1763. It still stands today, making it the oldest synagogue in the United States.
historicEvents.urlTitle: First Synagogue is built in America

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. George Lamphere was born about 1645, in Westerly, Kings, Rhode Island, British Colonial America. She had at least 5 sons and 5 daughters with George Lamphere.
